title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control · CI/CD Retrieval
summary: An operations handoff reconstructs release automation, npm trusted publishing, and change control from delayed run evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the multi-artifact release and publication policy under changed load. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-artifact release and publication policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Release Approval Separation Duty
steps.instruction:
review case — A review tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

Inspect the Ci Release Approval Separation Duty trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Release Approval Separation Duty.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, keep Ci Release Approval Separation Duty connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: For Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Release Approval Separation Duty precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: More manual approval steps always improve release safety.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
